stem: A ski slope drops at an angle of 53° below the horizontal. A skier travels 80 feet along the slope from the top of a ski run to the bottom. Using the values sin 53° ≈ 4/5, cos 53° ≈ 3/5, and tan 53° ≈ 4/3, what is the vertical drop, in feet?
explanation: The skier travels 80 feet along the slope (the hypotenuse), and the vertical drop is the side opposite the 53° angle. Using sin 53° = opposite/hypotenuse: vertical drop = 80 × sin 53° = 80 × (4/5) = 64 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used cos 53° instead of sin 53°, finding the horizontal distance instead of the vertical drop: 80 × (3/5) = 48.
distractorRationale.B: Used the reciprocal of tan 53° (i.e., cot 53° = 3/4) instead of sin 53°, computing 80 × (3/4) = 60.
distractorRationale.C: Correct: sin 53° = opposite/hypotenuse, so vertical drop = 80 × (4/5) = 64.
distractorRationale.D: Set up the sine ratio inverted, dividing the hypotenuse by sin 53° instead of multiplying: 80 ÷ (4/5) = 80 × (5/4) = 100.
